learningObjective: Understand that dragging a square perpendicular to its plane creates a cube with three dimensions. The aha moment: the cube is built by sweeping the square through space, and every position along the drag creates a cross-section of the cube.
ahaMoment: The cube isn't just appearing — it's being built by sweeping the square through space, and every position along the drag creates a cross-section of the cube

miniSpec.title: Drag Square Handle to Extrude Cube
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: A flat square sits on the screen with a handle or arrow pointing outward from its center. As you drag the handle away from the square, a 3D cube grows in real-time, stretching from the original square. Release to see the final cube with its dimensions labeled.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: Handle or arrow extending perpendicular from square's center; controls extrusion depth/distance
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: Semi-transparent cube volume appears between original square and dragged position, with connecting edges drawing from original square's corners to new position; grows continuously during drag
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Distance value updates numerically; intermediate rectangular faces forming the sides are visible with subtle shading; original square remains at starting position while copied square moves
miniSpec.trace_strategy: Ghost images of the square at previous positions fade gradually, showing the path of extrusion; original square remains solid to emphasize it as the base
miniSpec.aha_moment: The cube isn't just appearing — it's being built by sweeping the square through space, and every position along the drag creates a cross-section of the cube

modelVariables.cube3d_extrusionDepth: Current extrusion depth from 0 (flat square) to 1 (full cube), controlled by dragging the handle
modelVariables.cube3d_sideLength: Side length of the base square (constant, for display)
modelVariables.cube3d_crossSectionCount: Derived: number of ghost cross-sections shown during extrusion
modelVariables.cube3d_faces: Derived: number of faces visible (2 at start, 6 when complete)
modelVariables.cube3d_edges: Derived: number of edges visible (4 at start, 12 when complete)
modelVariables.cube3d_vertices: Derived: number of vertices visible (4 at start, 8 when complete)
modelVariables.cube3d_hasInteracted: Boolean: whether the student has started dragging

assertions.description: On load, a flat square is displayed with no extrusion, inviting the student to drag the handle
assertions.visualCheck: Flat amber square with a visible drag handle/arrow extending from its center, extrusion depth shows 0, prompt to drag visible

assertions.description: As the handle is dragged, a cube volume grows and ghost cross-sections appear
assertions.visualCheck: Semi-transparent cube volume extends halfway, connecting edges visible, ghost squares trace the path

assertions.description: At full extrusion, all 8 vertices, 12 edges, and 6 faces of the cube are visible
assertions.visualCheck: Complete cube with all dimensions labeled, depth value shows 1, all 8 vertices clearly marked

assertions.description: During extrusion, multiple ghost cross-sections appear showing the sweep path
assertions.visualCheck: Multiple faded ghost squares visible between base and current position, showing the square's journey through space

assertions.description: The original base square stays in place while only the front face moves with the drag
assertions.visualCheck: Original amber square remains at starting position (back face of cube), copied square moves to front, connecting edges form the four side faces

invariants.description: Vertices count doubles from 4 (square) to 8 (cube) as extrusion approaches 1
Volume grows linearly with extrusion depth
